SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter

Class AbstractPayloadSplitter<T,R>

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter.AbstractPayloadSplitter<T,R>
Type Parameters:
T - composite type — the type seen by the annotated method (e.g. List<Country>)
R - slice type — the type stored/recovered per individual entry (e.g. Country)
All Implemented Interfaces:
PayloadSplitter<T,R>
Direct Known Subclasses:
AbstractListPayloadSplitter
public abstract class AbstractPayloadSplitter<T,R> extends Object implements PayloadSplitter<T,R>
Template-method base class for PayloadSplitter implementations that removes the boilerplate of building StoreContext / RecoverContext slices and of merging recovered slices back into a composite result.

The three public PayloadSplitter operations are implemented here as fixed templates; subclasses supply only the four domain-specific hooks below. This keeps every concrete splitter focused on what a slice key is and how a composite is taken apart and put back together, never on the surrounding context plumbing.

Public operation → subclass hook(s) it delegates to
Public operationDelegates toPurpose
splitOnStore(StoreContext) splitIntoSlices(Object) then keyArgsForSlice(Object, StoreContext) per slice Break the composite payload into slices and derive each slice's key args.
splitOnRecover(RecoverContext) keyArgsToRecover(List, RecoverContext) Turn the aggregate method args into one arg-group (hence one recoverAll/find call) per slice.
merge(List) mergeSlices(List, List) Re-assemble the recovered slices into the composite payload and aggregate args.

Slice-key contract. The args produced by keyArgsForSlice(R, com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter.StoreContext<T>) on the store path and the args produced by keyArgsToRecover(java.util.List<java.lang.Object>, com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter.RecoverContext<T>) on the recover path must derive the same store key for the same entity. If they diverge, a slice is stored under one key and looked up under another, so recovery silently returns nothing.

For the common List<R> composite, prefer the ready-made AbstractListPayloadSplitter which fixes T = List<R> and supplies sensible defaults for the store split and merge.

Author:
Anand Manissery
See Also:
  • PayloadSplitter
  • AbstractListPayloadSplitter
  • MergeResult

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractPayloadSplitter

      public AbstractPayloadSplitter()

  • Method Details

    • splitOnStore

      public List<StoreContext<R>> splitOnStore(StoreContext<T> context)
      Splits the composite StoreContext into per-slice store contexts. Called once per failover store operation when a splitter is configured.

      Template: splits the composite payload via splitIntoSlices(Object), then for each slice builds a StoreContext whose args come from keyArgsForSlice(Object, StoreContext). The original Failover is carried through unchanged so every slice stores under the same domain/expiry config.

      Specified by:
      splitOnStore in interface PayloadSplitter<T,R>
      Parameters:
      context - composite context carrying the full payload and original args
      Returns:
      ordered list of per-slice contexts to store individually

    • splitOnRecover

      public List<RecoverContext<R>> splitOnRecover(RecoverContext<T> context)
      Splits the composite RecoverContext into per-slice recover contexts. Called once per failover recover operation when a splitter is configured.

      RecoverAll contract (findAll / no-ID-args scenario): When the method has no ID args, or Failover.recoverAll() is true, this method is called with the original (possibly empty) args. Each returned context is forwarded to FailoverHandler.recoverAll on the slice delegate — one store findAll call per context. Every context must set the correct slice type R via RecoverContext.clazz. Returning an empty list suppresses recovery and logs a warning.

      Note: When using the default DefaultFailoverHandler whose recoverAll ignores args and returns all entries by name, return exactly one placeholder context to avoid N-times duplication. Return multiple contexts only when the slice delegate's recoverAll partitions results by the supplied args.

      Template: turns the aggregate method args into one arg-group per slice via keyArgsToRecover(List, RecoverContext), then builds one RecoverContext per group. Each context is stamped with the slice type R (so the delegate recovers the correct type) and carries the original failover and cause through.

      Specified by:
      splitOnRecover in interface PayloadSplitter<T,R>
      Parameters:
      context - composite context carrying the aggregate args and cause
      Returns:
      ordered list of per-slice contexts to recover individually; each context drives one recoverAll call on the slice delegate

    • merge

      public RecoverContext<T> merge(List<RecoverContext<R>> recoverContexts)
      Merges individually recovered slice contexts back into a single composite result. The returned context's payload field is the final recovered value.

      Deduplication: When PayloadSplitter.splitOnRecover(com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter.RecoverContext<T>) returns N contexts in recoverAll mode and the slice delegate's recoverAll does not partition by args (e.g. the default DefaultFailoverHandler), all N calls return the same full set of entries — resulting in N-times duplicates in contexts. This method is the right place to deduplicate, since the implementation has full domain knowledge of the slice type R: 

      
 List<R> deduped = contexts.stream()
     .map(RecoverContext::getPayload)
     .filter(Objects::nonNull)
     .collect(Collectors.toMap(R::getId, r -> r, (a, b) -> a))
     .values().stream().toList();

      Null payloads: a context's payload may be null — a per-key cache miss (where the framework preserves positional alignment by passing the null through), an expired/missing entry on the recover-all path, or a slice that exceeded failover.scatter.timeout. This method owns the null policy: keep the null positionally (as the default per-id splitter does), or filter it out (as the recover-all dedup example below does). The framework only short-circuits to null when the entire contexts list is empty — in that case merge is not called at all.

      Template: collects the recovered payload and args of every slice (preserving order), hands them to mergeSlices(List, List), and wraps the resulting MergeResult in a composite RecoverContext stamped with the composite type T. The failover and cause are taken from the first slice.

      Slice payloads may be null (cache miss / expired / timed-out slice). The null policy — keep positionally, drop, or reject the whole composite — belongs to mergeSlices(List, List), not here. The framework only short-circuits to null when the slice list is empty, in which case this method is not called.

      Specified by:
      merge in interface PayloadSplitter<T,R>
      Parameters:
      recoverContexts - per-slice contexts after each slice's payload has been recovered; never empty (the framework skips merge on an empty list)
      Returns:
      composite context whose payload is the merged result

    • keyArgsForSlice

      protected abstract List<Object> keyArgsForSlice(R payload, StoreContext<T> context)
      Derives the store-key args for a single slice on the store path.

      The returned list is fed to the configured KeyGenerator, so it must produce the same key that a direct single-entity call would (e.g. List.of(payload.getId())). It must also match what keyArgsToRecover(List, RecoverContext) produces per slice on the recover path, otherwise stored and recovered keys diverge.

      Parameters:
      payload - the individual slice being stored
      context - the composite store context (full payload + original method args)
      Returns:
      key args identifying payload; typically a single-element list of the entity id

    • splitIntoSlices

      protected abstract List<R> splitIntoSlices(T payload)
      Breaks the composite payload into individual slices on the store path.

      One StoreContext is built per returned slice. For a List<R> composite this is the identity (each element is a slice) — see AbstractListPayloadSplitter.splitIntoSlices(List).

      Parameters:
      payload - the composite payload returned by the annotated method
      Returns:
      the slices to store individually, in order

    • keyArgsToRecover

      protected abstract List<List<Object>> keyArgsToRecover(List<Object> args, RecoverContext<T> context)
      Splits the aggregate method args into one arg-group per slice on the recover path.

      Each returned List<Object> drives exactly one recover/find call on the slice delegate and must derive the same key keyArgsForSlice(Object, StoreContext) produced when storing.

      Shapes by scenario (assuming the slice key is the entity id):

      • findAll() — no args: return a single group List.of(args) (the default in AbstractListPayloadSplitter); the delegate recovers all slices by name.
      • findByIdsIn(List<Long> ids): return one group per id — ids.stream().map(List::of).toList().
      • findByStringIdsIn(String csv): split the CSV, then one group per id.
      • with extra filter args (active, region, ...): ignore the filters for keying — they are not entity identity — and split only the id portion.
      Parameters:
      args - the original aggregate method args (possibly empty for findAll())
      context - the composite recover context (failover, args, cause)
      Returns:
      one arg-group per slice to recover; an empty list suppresses recovery

    • mergeSlices

      protected abstract MergeResult<T> mergeSlices(List<R> payloads, List<List<Object>> args)
      Re-assembles the recovered slices into the composite payload on the recover path.

      Owns the null/partial-recovery policy: a slice payload may be null (cache miss, expired, or timed-out). Choose to keep nulls positionally, drop them, deduplicate, or reject the whole composite — this method has the domain knowledge to decide. It also owns how the per-slice args are aggregated back into the composite args.

      Parameters:
      payloads - recovered slice payloads in slice order; entries may be null
      args - the per-slice arg-groups in the same order as payloads
      Returns:
      the merged composite payload plus aggregated args, wrapped in a MergeResult
